Name:
efiL4zaggiN
(Collaboration with N.W.A.)
(Niggaz4Life)
Track Number / Info:
(01/25/1988) (Straight Outta Compton officially releases)
(05/28/1991) (efiL4zaggiN officially releases)
Streaming: efiL4zaggiN (Niggaz4Life spelled backwards) is the second and final studio album by N.W.A., and the only N.W.A. album to not feature Ice Cube as a group member. The project is notable for its extremely dark sound, lyrics and subject matter such as rape and mutilation, teetering on horrorcore at some points. This is in stark contrast to other N.W.A. projects which are somewhat lighter in tone.

Track Number / Info:

(08/??/2004) (Dre gives up on Detox)
(11/??/2004) (Dre announces that he's back to working on Detox)
Streaming: The first era of Detox. Off the heels from 2001 Dre took some time to help bring up Eminem and Xzibit's albums and work with other artists, of course while working on his then supposed to be third album, Detox. Dre himself would state that Detox would be a "hip hop musical" of sorts, conceptualized about a hitman. However, the concept was scrapped a couple years after. Not much material has leaked from this era but at the time Dre was promoting this as his final album before retiring (even claiming that the verse he recorded for Where I'm From would be his last verse ever). In 2004, Dre also mentioned he wanted to put out up to 12 singles for the album as well, however later that year he seems to have restarted development once again. Songs such as Want Me Back would leave a legacy for Dre.

(11/??/2004) (Dre announces that he's back to working on Detox)
(??/??/2005) (The D.O.C. reunites with Dre and starts working on Detox)
(09/??/2007) (Dr. Dre flies out to Detroit to help Eminem finish his mixtape)
Streaming: Detox V2 was quite the busy time for Dre and was the start of the many delays that would happen to Detox. During this time Dre would start to branch out for working with artists and would change up the Detox sound. This was also the era when his working relationship with JAY-Z really came to a start. We dont have many leaks from this time but from what we know this was a promising iteration of Detox.

Name: Detox [V3]
Track Number / Info:
(09/??/2007) (Dr. Dre flies out to Detroit to help Eminem finish his mixtape)
(03/16/2009) (Eminem, Dr. Dre & 50 Cent fly to Vegas to shoot the We Made You music video)
Streaming: One of the more lesser heard from eras of Detox is the third iteration, it was mostly carried by Bishop Lamont & Hayes with Slim The Mobster only getting involved near the end of this version around 2007. Dre was noticeably quiet on the released music side with not much content involving his name coming out. This version of Detox would also start to introduce different producers into the sound such as Jake One and DJ Khalil along with working with other artists such as T.I. & Rain 910. Lastly, this era would also introduce Sly Pyper, Dre's most underrated Aftermath artist.

Name: Detox [V4]
Track Number / Info:
(03/16/2009) (Eminem, Dr. Dre & 50 Cent fly to Vegas to shoot the We Made You music video)
(11/14/2011) (Dre announces a break from solo music)
Streaming: The most wanted version of Detox. Coming out of "Relapse" sessions "Detox" is finally put into full focus by Dr. Dre, and the most extensive sessions are held through this period. Singles are released and the album comes the closest it ever did to releasing, but ultimately burns out and Dre steps back from working on his own music again in 2011.

Name: Singles

I Need A Doctor
(feat. Eminem & Skylar Grey) (prod. Alex Da Kid)
Track Number / Info: Released as a teaser for Detox but was not intended for placement on the album. The song focuses on Eminem calling for Dre to return to music. Alex Da Kid originally had the idea to have Lady Gaga record the hook, but Eminem insisted Skylar be kept.

Name:
Kush
(feat. Snoop Dogg & Akon) (add. Sly Pyper, Kobe, Slim The Mobster & Blaqthoven) (prod. DJ Khalil, Bēkon & Dr. Dre)
Track Number / Info: Leaked shortly before release. Was intended as a "warm up" single and not actually a single for Detox as according to Dre. Also according to Dre, this song wasn't representative of the sound for Detox and was actually a one off in terms of subject matter and sound. Includes chords by Bēkon. Although he doesn't have any listed credit, DJ Khalil has mentioned that Dre did add production to the song, adding a hi-hat, shaker, and layering to the kick and snare. Khalil described Dre as adding more "movement" to the beat.

Name:
Detox [V5]
(Thanks To My Patients For Your Patience)
Track Number / Info:
(11/14/2011) (Dre announces a break from solo music)
(08/05/2014) (Princple photography for Straight Outta Compton begins)
Streaming: Taking a step back from solo music Dre focuses heavily on ushering in a new era of Afermath artists while giving some side time to another version of Detox. From this stretch of time emerges Jon Connor, Kendrick Lamar and the formation of the ICU. Though this version of Detox didnt bear much fruit it morphed into what we now know as Compton.

Track Number / Info:
(??/??/2016) (Compton Deluxe is scrapped)
(03/19/2020) (California is placed under lockdown due to COVID-19, ushering in a new era of Detox)
Streaming: Following the release of Compton, Dr. Dre enters a semi-retirement from solo music, mainly focusing on supporting new Aftermath signees and only sparatically working on music himself. However, Detox was still being made (being brought back after Compton released), but wasn't the main focus. In 2018, ESPN reporter Chris Haynes asked Dr. Dre is Detox would be coming out, and Dre's response to him was "I'm working on a couple songs right now. We'll see", meaning the title was still likely in use.

(feat. Dr. Dre & Ty Dolla $ign) (prod. Swizz Beatz & Dr. Dre)

Dre first teased the song in a Drink Champs interview promoting "Missionary", in which he revealed he'd be adding a feature and production to a Swizz Beatz-produced song for Xzibit's upcoming album. Dre's verse is from early November 2024 and Xzibit's verse is likely from sometime around late 2024 or from the same session as Dre's verse since he mentions signing to Greenback and Kendrick Lamar's "Not Like Us".
